race.name: Gran Fondo Estonia (Tallinn-Tartu)
race.tagline: A discontinued Baltic point-to-point — not a race, a controlled group ride.

vitals.date: Discontinued — traditionally held around August 20; last confirmed edition 2019
vitals.date_specific: Last confirmed edition: 2019. A 2020 edition was planned but no evidence it occurred; absent from Estonia's 2026 road calendar.

climate.description: Late-August Estonia: mild Baltic summer conditions along the Tallinn-Tartu corridor.

terrain.surface: Mostly paved roads, with one short gravel sector and a cobbled finishing climb in Tartu

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 51. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 50. FLAG (wave 10, not applied): the identity correction changed the verified distance from a fabricated 130.4km to a real ~230km, which sits well above the distance=3 rubric band; field_depth=2 also assumes rankings this non-competitive group ride never had. Numbers left unchanged pending human review — this entire profile may warrant a full rescore or removal given the identity/defunct findings.

course_description.character: IMPORTANT — this profile was mislabeled 'Tallinn Gran Fondo' with fabricated-looking vitals (130.4 km, a fixed August 15 date) that match no verifiable event. The real event behind this entry is Gran Fondo Estonia, a point-to-point ride between Tallinn and Tartu, roughly 230-234 km depending on the year, traditionally held around August 20. It was a controlled, non-competitive group ride, not a timed race — riders moved in escorted pace groups with food, medical, and mechanical support, and a roughly 100-115 km Medio Fondo option ended around Tamsalu rather than continuing to Tartu. The route ran mostly flat from Tallinn to Tamsalu before hillier terrain through the Pandivere Upland; the documented 2015 route included a short (~1 km, ~10% grade) climb at Emumägi, one short gravel sector despite the 'road' framing, and a narrow cobbled finishing climb beneath Tartu's Angel's Bridge (Inglisild). No edition has been confirmed since 2019 — a planned 2020 edition under a new organizer (MTÜ Sportlik Eesti) has no evidence it ran, and the event is absent from Estonia's current road-cycling calendar.
course_description.signature_challenge: Covering 230+ km point-to-point in an escorted pace group, capped by a narrow, cobbled finishing climb at Tartu's Angel's Bridge that riders expecting a standard paved fondo weren't necessarily braced for.
suffering_zones: The Emumägi climb: short but genuinely steep (~10%) in an otherwise mostly flat route.
The Tartu finish: a constricted cobbled climb beneath Angel's Bridge — an unusual, technical finale for what was marketed as a road event.

logistics.airport: Tallinn (TLL) for the start; Tartu (TAY) near the finish.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Abundant hotels in Tallinn's historic Old Town for a pre-event stay; this is moot for the event itself, which is discontinued.
logistics.food: Historically fully supported with food stops along the route, consistent with its controlled-group-ride format.

history.origin_story: First held in 2015 and organized by MTÜ TriSmile, led by former triathlete Ain-Alar Juhanson, with Hawaii Express as title sponsor. The 2017 edition was described locally as the third running. Direction reversed to Tartu-Tallinn in 2018, then returned to Tallinn-Tartu in 2019 — the last confirmed edition. A planned 2020 edition was handed to a different organizer (MTÜ Sportlik Eesti); no evidence it took place.

biased_opinion.summary: There is no evidence a 'Tallinn Gran Fondo' matching this profile's vitals (130.4 km, fixed August 15 date) ever existed. What the underlying research and video footage actually document is Gran Fondo Estonia, a real, roughly 230 km Tallinn-to-Tartu controlled group ride that ran from 2015 through at least 2019 under sponsor Hawaii Express and organizer MTÜ TriSmile. It was never a timed race — riders moved in escorted pace groups — and it included a short gravel sector and a narrow cobbled Tartu finish despite being marketed as a road event. No edition has been confirmed since 2019, and it's absent from Estonia's current cycling calendar.
strengths: A genuine, multi-year event (2015-2019 confirmed) with real sponsor and organizer backing, not a fabrication
Fully supported format with food, medical, and mechanical support for a demanding 230+ km point-to-point
A real geographic through-route connecting Estonia's two largest cities, with some genuine terrain variety (Pandivere hills, the Emumägi climb)
weaknesses: Discontinued — no confirmed edition since 2019, absent from Estonia's current road calendar
Never a competitive, timed race despite gran-fondo framing — it was an escorted group ride
Included an unadvertised gravel sector and a technical cobbled finish, a mismatch for riders expecting a straightforward paved fondo
This database profile itself was previously mislabeled with unverifiable vitals under the wrong name — a reminder to double-check any 'Tallinn Gran Fondo' listing you find elsewhere
biased_opinion.bottom_line: This entry exists as a historical correction, not a recommendation — no edition of the event it actually describes has been confirmed since 2019. If you want a real, currently active Baltic road event, look elsewhere in Estonia or Latvia's active calendars rather than searching for this one.

final_verdict.one_liner: A discontinued Tallinn-Tartu group ride, previously mislabeled in this database as an active gran fondo that never matched the real event.
final_verdict.should_you_race: You can't — no edition has run since 2019 and it's absent from Estonia's current calendar. If a similarly named event resurfaces, verify it independently before trusting any figures carried over from this profile's history.
final_verdict.alternatives: For an active Estonian/Baltic road cycling option, check Estonia's current national federation (EJL) event calendar directly rather than relying on this listing.

eligibility.notes: SUPERSEDES prior 'unknown' status. The real event behind this profile, Gran Fondo Estonia (Tallinn-Tartu), last confirmed in 2019; a planned 2020 edition under a new organizer has no evidence it occurred, and no event by this or a related name appears on Estonia's current (EJL) road-cycling calendar. Verified 2026-07-24 via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research, editorial wave 10.
eligibility.identity_correction: IDENTITY CORRECTION (2026-07-24): this profile was mislabeled 'Tallinn Gran Fondo' with fabricated-looking vitals (130.4 km, fixed August 15 date) matching no verifiable event. The content and attached video footage actually describe Gran Fondo Estonia, a real ~230km Tallinn-Tartu controlled group ride (2015-2019, organizer MTÜ TriSmile, sponsor Hawaii Express). It is NOT the same event as the Tallinn-Tartu GP stage of the Tour of Estonia, a separate licensed professional stage race — that footage was purged from youtube_data. Renamed race.name/display_name to 'Gran Fondo Estonia (Tallinn-Tartu)' to match; slug left unchanged (tallinn-gran-fondo) to avoid breaking existing URLs.
